title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Global Box Office Performance
text: James Cameron's third Avatar installment reached a global box office total of $1.48 billion, comprising $400 million domestically and $1.07 billion internationally. The film was produced with a $400 million budget. The threequel significantly underperformed compared to earlier installments. The original Avatar earned $2.9 billion globally, while Avatar: The Way of Water grossed $2.3 billion. Fire and Ash dropped out of the domestic top 10 after 10 weeks—considerably shorter than the first film's 14-week run or the sequel's 15-week tenure. The film received less favorable reviews, earning a 66% score on Rotten Tomatoes. Critics noted that Fire and Ash repeats the narrative beats of its predecessors to frustrating effect, despite maintaining strong visual effects. Both previous Avatar films received Best Picture Oscar nominations, an achievement this installment failed to match. The underperformance has raised questions about the franchise's viability, particularly given Cameron's stated interest in exploring other projects and the substantial time and financial commitments required for Avatar productions. The film earned approximately $4 million globally in its final tracked weekend, suggesting the $1.5 billion threshold is unlikely to be reached.
